documentation.text:
@title  CPTStore
 @notice On-chain repository for LUNA Bayesian Network parameters.
         Stores priors P(PPH=true) and P(PPR=true), and Conditional
         Probability Table (CPT) entries for four binary evidence nodes
         (GPS, PC, PMD, PR).  All probabilities are scaled integers
         in [0, SCALE].
 @dev    Design invariants (referenced in paper Section IV-A):
         1. Every parameter write is range-checked: 0 <= p <= SCALE.
         2. Every parameter write emits an indexed event, enabling full
            reconstruction of BN parameter history from the ledger.
         3. Write access is restricted to the contract owner (deployer).
         4. A bnInstanceId fingerprint is recomputed lazily (on the first
            read after any write), keeping per-write gas O(1) in storage
            writes and making initialization cost O(N_e) in SSTORE ops.
         5. getCPTSnapshot() returns all 18 parameters + bnInstanceId in
            a single call, reducing oracle BN reconstruction to 1 RPC.
         6. block.number is available in every transaction receipt;
            the oracle records it externally to bind each posterior
            submission to the parameter state at reconstruction time.

@notice Number of binary evidence nodes supported.
         0 = GPS, 1 = PC, 2 = PMD, 3 = PR.

@notice Contract owner; the only address permitted to write
         parameters.  Set to msg.sender at deployment.

@notice Scaled prior P(PPH = true).
         PPH and PPR are modeled as marginally independent root
         nodes; they need not sum to SCALE.  Mutual exclusivity
         of claim types is enforced at the application layer.

@notice keccak256 fingerprint of the current parameter state.
         Recomputed lazily after any write.  The oracle records
         this value alongside each posterior submission so that
         auditors can verify which parameter version was active.
@notice Emitted when contract ownership is transferred.

@notice Deploys CPTStore.
         Priors are initialised to neutral values (0.5 / 0.5).
         Call setPriors() and setEvidenceCPT() after deployment
         to configure the desired parameter set before any
         inference submissions are accepted.

@notice Transfers contract ownership to newOwner.
 @param  newOwner  Address of the new owner.  Must be non-zero.

@dev Recomputes bnInstanceId from the full current parameter
      state and clears the dirty flag.  Called immediately after
      every write so that bnInstanceId is always current when
      read by external callers or emitted in events.

devdoc.details: Design invariants (referenced in paper Section IV-A):         1. Every parameter write is range-checked: 0 <= p <= SCALE.         2. Every parameter write emits an indexed event, enabling full            reconstruction of BN parameter history from the ledger.         3. Write access is restricted to the contract owner (deployer).         4. A bnInstanceId fingerprint is recomputed lazily (on the first            read after any write), keeping per-write gas O(1) in storage            writes and making initialization cost O(N_e) in SSTORE ops.         5. getCPTSnapshot() returns all 18 parameters + bnInstanceId in            a single call, reducing oracle BN reconstruction to 1 RPC.         6. block.number is available in every transaction receipt;            the oracle records it externally to bind each posterior            submission to the parameter state at reconstruction time.

getCPTSnapshot().details: cpts layout (length 16):         cpts[ i*4 + pphState*2 + pprState ]           = P(evidence_i = true | PPH=pphState, PPR=pprState)         for i in [0, NUM_EVIDENCE), pphState/pprState in {0,1}.

setEvidenceCPT(uint8,uint8,uint8,uint256.details: Gas cost per call: O(1) SSTOREs (one data slot + dirty flag).         bnInstanceId is recomputed once on the subsequent read,         keeping initialization cost O(N_e) in SSTORE operations.

setPriors(uint256,uint256).details: Each value is independently range-checked.  The two         priors need not sum to SCALE; see contract-level note         on marginal independence.

params.newOwner: Address of the new owner.  Must be non-zero.

_dirty.details: Dirty flag for lazy bnInstanceId recomputation.         Set to true on every parameter write; cleared when         bnInstanceId is recomputed in _refreshIfDirty().

evidenceTrueCPT.details: CPT storage: evidenceTrueCPT[i][pph][ppr] =         P(evidence_i = true | PPH = pph, PPR = ppr), scaled.         Indices: i in [0, NUM_EVIDENCE), pph/ppr in {0, 1}.

devdoc.title: CPTStore

EvidenceCPTUpdated(uint8,uint8,uint8,uin.notice: Emitted on every CPT entry update.         Three indexed fields (EVM topic limit) allow filtering         by evidence node and parent configuration.         bnInstanceId is stored in event data (not indexed) and         is readable via log data scan or getCPTSnapshot().

OwnershipTransferred(address,address).notice: Emitted when contract ownership is transferred.

PriorsUpdated(uint256,uint256,bytes32).notice: Emitted on every prior update.         Indexed on bnInstanceId so callers can filter parameter         history by snapshot.

NUM_EVIDENCE().notice: Number of binary evidence nodes supported.         0 = GPS, 1 = PC, 2 = PMD, 3 = PR.

SCALE().notice: Global scale factor.  All probabilities p in [0,1] are         stored as round(p * SCALE).  Absolute rounding error is         bounded by 1 / (2 * SCALE) = 5e-7.

bnInstanceId().notice: keccak256 fingerprint of the current parameter state.         Recomputed lazily after any write.  The oracle records         this value alongside each posterior submission so that         auditors can verify which parameter version was active.

constructor.notice: Deploys CPTStore.         Priors are initialised to neutral values (0.5 / 0.5).         Call setPriors() and setEvidenceCPT() after deployment         to configure the desired parameter set before any         inference submissions are accepted.

getCPTSnapshot().notice: Returns all 18 BN parameters and the current bnInstanceId         in a single call, reducing off-chain BN reconstruction         to one RPC round-trip.

owner().notice: Contract owner; the only address permitted to write         parameters.  Set to msg.sender at deployment.

priorPPH().notice: Scaled prior P(PPH = true).         PPH and PPR are modeled as marginally independent root         nodes; they need not sum to SCALE.  Mutual exclusivity         of claim types is enforced at the application layer.

setEvidenceCPT(uint8,uint8,uint8,uint256.notice: Sets one CPT entry:         P(evidence_evidenceIndex = true | PPH=pphState, PPR=pprState).

userdoc.notice: On-chain repository for LUNA Bayesian Network parameters.         Stores priors P(PPH=true) and P(PPR=true), and Conditional         Probability Table (CPT) entries for four binary evidence nodes         (GPS, PC, PMD, PR).  All probabilities are scaled integers         in [0, SCALE].
